utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next patient is christine hernandez , uh , date of birth is january 13th , 1982 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , miss christine , how are you doing today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good , thanks . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm pretty good . so it looks like you've completed the covid vaccine , that's great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i did .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: anything new since your last visit ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i did all the tests that you had recommended me to take . i have n't been able to take the thyroid medicine , the one that you prescribed , as i'm still taking my old one . um , the price was a little high on the new one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so did ... did you try the coupon that i gave you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did not try the coupon , uh , there was a charge of $ 75 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well , next time that ... that coupon should help , and it should only be about $ 3 .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , um ... i do n't have it , do you happen to have another one you can give me ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yep , right here .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: wonderful , thank you so much , and ... and then the gel , they are charging me $ 100 for it . so , i do n't know if this is because it's a ... it's wal-mart , or if i should try somewhere else , or ... maybe you know how or where i can get it cheaper .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , let's try something else , um ... sometimes it can be cheaper if we just prescribe you the individual ingredients of a medication , rather than the , the combined medication itself .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that would be great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , that's clindamycin gel and benzoyl peroxide , uh , maybe by doing them separately , they could be a lot cheaper . so , that we can do . the unithroid , with the discount code , should only be about $ 9 for 90 days .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that would be great . yeah , they were charging me $ 75 , and i just could n't pay that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: maybe we'll try different pharmacy , as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so , do you think that my weight gain could have been the birth control that i was taking before that caused it ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: maybe . i do n't really see an endocrine cause for it , at least , so i would need to see the , the hyperandrogynism or high testosterone . or , a high dhea , to cause acne , or hair growth , or any of that stuff . but , the numbers are n't showing up out of range .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i really do n't see any endocrine cause for it , like i said . your growth hormone was fine , but we definitely want to and need to treat it . um , i do n't know if we talked about maybe a little weight loss study .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you mentioned the weight loss study , and you mentioned that i have some meal plans , um , that you had given me . i still have those , too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you tried to make any changes in the diet since the last time we spoke ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been trying to get better . i will start back at the gym in july , because of my contract , i had to put a hold on it until then .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , i do want to start doing that . i will be a little freer since , um , i'll be on vacation after july 8th .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then my cousin was telling me to ask you about cla , because it's supposed to help your metabolism . is that okay to take ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , i'm not sure . what is c , cla ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm not sure what it is , either .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well , i'm unfamiliar with it , so ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i also have a coworker who has a thyroid issue too , and she suggested to try chromium for weight loss .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , that likely will not help too much . you can try either , if you really want to , but then ... it will not accept you into the weight loss study if you try those two .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: chromium is just a supplement and it wo n't help that much .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it wo n't , okay , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it wo n't hurt ... okay , i should n't say that it wo n't hurt , but , it also wo n't help that much . so , it's up to you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and so , my cousin also suggested amino acids , and that i might find them in certain foods , i guess , for my workout .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . amino acids are fine , they wo n't , wo n't really help with weight loss either , but it might help , uh , you replenish , and just kind of , feel hydrated .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . are they proteins ? um , my cousin said she had lost some weight , and has been working out every day , but she does n't work , so ... i do n't know .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , amino acids are what make up the protein , which is in any food you eat , with any protein . so , meats , dairy , nuts , any of that sort of thing .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , thank you . got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , are you allergic to any medications ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not that i know of .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . is your s- skin pretty sensitive ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , yeah , my size , i will start getting rashes , with different products .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and have you ever tried clindamycin topical , as an antibiotic for your acne ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i've never tried anything for it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . we might give you some of that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and i also want to mention that my feet do swell up a lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i'm ... let me take a look at that for just a moment . um , any constipation ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i also do have that problem .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . mira- miralax will definitely help with that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , yes , my doctor did also recommend that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . all right , let's do an exam real quick . please have a seat on this table and i'll listen to your lungs and heart .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , deep breath . all right , again .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let me take a look at your feet and ankles .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , they look okay right now , certainly let your doctor know about this if it gets any worse or reoccurs .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i will do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now , let's go over your lab work . so , when you took that pill , the dexamethasone test , you passed , which means you do n't have cushing's syndrome . on that test , at least . the salivary cortisol , though , unless you did one wrong ... two of them were completely normal and one was abnormal , so , we might need to repeat that in the future .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that's okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so , your cholesterol was quite high . the total cholesterol was 222 . the good cholesterol was about 44 . the bad was 153 , and it should be less than 100 . the non-hdl was about 178 , and it should be less than 130 . the good cholesterol should be over 50 , and it was 44 . so , your screen for diabetes is ... was fine . you do have a vitamin d deficiency , and , i do n't know if we started the vitamin d yet , or not .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , we did . i- i do need to take one today , though .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , i also checked a lot of other pituitary hormones , iron levels ... everything else seemed to be pretty good , and in decent range .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds great . so , i wanted to also show you my liver enzymes , um , because i have n't come back since then ... but i was also happy , because one of them was back to normal .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . let's see them .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so , the one that's 30 , that was almost 200 not so long ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , your alt was about 128 .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , and , and back in october was 254 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , this is much better .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , great . and then it dropped in january , and then it dropped a little more in march , since i stopped taking the medicine in december .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that's good . so ... i'm proud of you with the course of your labs , so before i forget , i'm going to , uh , just put your labs into the computer today , and i wo n't be checking your vitamin d level for some time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so , with the thyroid , and the low vitamin d , does that always happen together ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , i do have a lot of people that have thyroid , thyroid issues and they have vitamin d deficiency .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: this is what i'm , um , i'm going to do . i'm going to put , print out your prescriptions , so you can shop around at the pharmacies and see if you can find better prices .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that way i can go ask them and try cvs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , that sounds like a plan .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , good . so , the weight loss study that you mentioned , when does that start ? or , how does that work ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we are about to start , as we just got approval last week , and we are just waiting on our paperwork so we can get started .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , and what's involved with that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , it'll involve you receiving a medication which has been used for diabetes treatment , and it works mostly in the gut on satiety , or satiety hormones . um , the most common side effects are going to be nausea , vomiting , diarrhea and constipation . they are s- uh , six arms , to the study . one is a placebo , the other ones are a , various as ... various dosages of the medication , excuse me . um , you would receive an injection once a week . also , keep in mind that most of the weight loss medications are not covered by insurance .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , it's a way of getting them , but , the odds of getting one of the arms with the medication that are in your favor , right , might be only one out of five of our hundred patients that we have on the list for the study that will receive the placebo .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does that make sense ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we do expect pretty big weight loss , because of what we learn in diabetes study . so , it's a year long , uh , process , and it's an injection once a week . you come in weekly for the first four , five weeks , i believe . and then , after that , it's once a month . you do get a stipend for partici- for participating in the study , and parking is validated , and whatever else that you need for the study .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , do you know how much the stipend is ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , i will have to double check for you , and , you do n't have to be my patient , you just have to meet the criteria . so the criteria is a bmi greater than 30 , if you do n't have any other medical condition . or , a bmi greater than 27 , if you do have another medical condition , like your cholesterol . um , a bmi greater than 27 would quali- uh , qualify you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have a friend who might be interested , and she does have diabetes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if she has d- diabetes , then she wo n't qualify .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , you ca n't if you ... if you have diabetes , got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: correct . yeah , the only thing that , um , they can not have , really , is diabetes . so , either a psychiatric disease , or schizophrenia , bipolar , things like that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but , if they have hypertension , high cholesterol , things like that ... they can definitely sign up .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and they can , okay . thank you for explaining that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: of course . so , do you want me to try to get you into that study ? or , would you just like to try , me to prescribe something ? it's kind of up to you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think i'll just wait for a little bit now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , sounds good . i'll give you the information for the research , it's just in my office . um , it is a different phone number , though . so then , if you're interested , just call us within a month , because i do n't know how long , uh , the , the wait will be .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , will do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: perfect . so , let me go grab your discount card for the unithroid . um , when you go in to activate it , the instructions are on this card , and then you use your insurance ... then , show them this , and ask how much it'll cost . if it's too expensive , just let me know .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i will . thank you so much for your help on that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . then , what i did is , i gave you a topical antibiotic , plus i gave you the benzoyl peroxide . so , the peroxide may bleach your sheets , but , you want to make sure to take it and apply it at night , so you do n't have a reaction from the sun during the day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i can do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but , you do also want to make sure that you do n't mess up your sheets .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so , that's that . and then , let's see how you do on the other medications . i think this will , this will get better . in the meantime , a low-carb diet , avoid alcohol and fatty foods , and low chole- cholesterol foods .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and again , once you finish your dose of vitamin d , for the vitamin d deficiency , you're gon na start with the 2000iu daily , so that you're able to maintain those levels . sound good ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that sounds great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i really think your liver enzymes are going to get better once you lose the weight , though .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that would be great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: since we stopped your birth control , we can try once called phexxi , which is kind of like a spermicide , basically .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you just apply it before intercourse .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if you need some , uh , just let me know .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i will . i'll let you know .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , perfect . so , stay put for me now . i'm going to go see if they have discount samples , and bring you that prescription . and then , i'm going to order the labs for next time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , great , thank you so much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , under the plan , under abnormal liver enzymes , they have improved since discontinuation of her birth control . under abnormal weight gain , her dexamethasone suppression test was normal . two out of three salivary cortisol tests were normal , not consistent with cushing's , and therefore we're ruling out cushing's . under her hirsutism , her androgen levels were normal . for the acne vulgaris , the epiduo was not covered , so we'll try benzoyl peroxide with clindamycin , and remove the previous information . on the hyperthyroidism , we'll print out her prescriptions . unithroid should be better priced with the discount card , and we'll repeat levels of everything before next visit . thanks .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 39-year-old female.
The patient has not started the new thyroid medication due to high cost and continues taking her previous medication.
The doctor asks if the patient attempted to use the provided discount coupon for her thyroid medication.
The patient reports the thyroid medication cost $75 and she did not use the coupon.
The patient reports the prescribed acne gel cost $100.
The doctor advises prescribing clindamycin gel and benzoyl peroxide separately to reduce costs.
Weight gain
The patient asks if her discontinued oral contraceptive caused her weight gain.
The doctor states there is no endocrine cause for the patient's weight gain, acne, or hirsutism as androgen levels are normal.
The doctor notes the patient's growth hormone levels are normal.
The patient plans to resume her gym membership in July.
The patient asks if CLA is safe and effective for metabolism and weight loss.
The patient asks if chromium is effective for weight loss.
The doctor advises that chromium and CLA will not significantly help with weight loss and would disqualify the patient from the weight loss study.
The patient asks if amino acids are effective for weight loss.
The doctor states amino acids do not aid weight loss but may help with hydration and replenishment.
The doctor asks if the patient has any medication allergies.
The patient denies any known medication allergies.
The doctor asks if the patient has sensitive skin.
The patient reports sensitive skin and developing rashes with various products.
The doctor asks if the patient has previously used topical clindamycin for acne.
The patient denies previous treatment for acne.
The patient reports swelling in her feet.
The doctor asks if the patient suffers from constipation.
The patient confirms she suffers from constipation.
The doctor recommends MiraLAX for constipation.
The doctor performs a physical exam, listening to the patient's heart and lungs.
The doctor examines the patient's feet and ankles.
The doctor finds the patient's feet and ankles appear normal.
The doctor reports the Dexamethasone Suppression Test was normal and Cushing's Syndrome is ruled out, though salivary cortisol tests were inconsistent.
The doctor reviews lipid panel results showing high cholesterol and confirms a vitamin D deficiency.
Liver enzymes
The patient reports improvement in her liver enzymes.
The doctor notes the patient's ALT level was 128.
The patient asks for details regarding the weight loss study.
The doctor explains the weight loss study involves a weekly injection, potential side effects like nausea and constipation, and requires a specific BMI.
The doctor outlines the BMI criteria for the weight loss study.
The doctor instructs the patient to call within a month if she decides to participate in the weight loss study.
The doctor prescribes topical antibiotic and benzoyl peroxide for acne, advising application at night.
The doctor recommends a low-carb, low-cholesterol diet and avoiding alcohol and fatty foods.
The doctor instructs the patient to start 2000 IU of vitamin D daily after finishing her current high-dose prescription.
The doctor recommends Phexxi as an alternative birth control method.
The doctor will order labs for the next visit.
